title: Generics, Type Sets, Constraints, Inference, and Iteration · Systems Transfer
summary: A collection library needs reusable operations without reflection, lost relationships, over-broad constraints, or unreadable APIs. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a constrained reusable collection library with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.
Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
